sections.title: DEFINITION OF PRODUCE
sections.text: This Standard applies to commercial varieties of avocados grown from Persea americana Mill., of the Lauraceae family, to be supplied fresh to the consumer, after preparation and packaging. Parthenocarpic fruit and avocados for industrial processing are excluded.
sections.retrieval_text:
DEFINITION OF PRODUCE
number=1
level=1
This Standard applies to commercial varieties of avocados grown from Persea americana Mill., of the Lauraceae family, to be supplied fresh to the consumer, after preparation and packaging. Parthenocarpic fruit and avocados for industrial processing are excluded.

page_spans.text: This Standard applies to commercial varieties of avocados grown from Persea americana Mill., of the Lauraceae family, to be supplied fresh to the consumer, after preparation and packaging. Parthenocarpic fruit and avocados for industrial processing are excluded.

sections.title: The avocados must have reached a stage of physiological development which will ensure the completion of the ripening process, in accordance with criteria proper to the variety and to the area in which they are grown.
path_titles: The avocados must have reached a stage of physiological development which will ensure the completion of the ripening process, in accordance with criteria proper to the variety and to the area in which they are grown.
sections.text:
The mature fruit should be free of bitterness. 
The development and condition of the avocados must be such as to enable them:  
­ to withstand transport and handling; and 
­ to arrive in satisfactory condition at the place of destination.

sections.title: Maturity requirements
sections.text:
The fruit should have a minimum dry matter content 1 at the harvest, according to the variety, to be measured by drying to constant weight: 
 21 % for the variety Hass; 
 20 % for the varieties Torres, Fuerte, Pinkerton, Edranol and Reed.  
Other varieties including Antillean/West Indian/Guatemalan may show a lower dry matter content.

sections.title: “Extra” Class
sections.text: Avocados in this class must be of superior quality. They must be characteristic of the variety. They must be free of defects, with the exception of very slight superficial defects, provided these do not affect the general appearance of the produce, the quality, the keeping quality and presentation in the package. If present, the stalk must be intact.

sections.title: Class I
sections.text:
Avocados in this class must be of good quality. They must be characteristic of the variety. The following slight defects, however, may be allowed, provided these do not affect the general appearan ce of the produce, the quality, the keeping quality and presentation in the package: 
- slight defects in shape and colouring; 
- slight skin defects (corkiness, healed lenticels) and sunburn; the maximum total area should not exceed 4 cm2. 
The defects must not, in any case, affect the flesh of the fruit. 
The stalk, if present, may be slightly damaged.

page_spans.text:
Avocados in this class must be of good quality. They must be characteristic of the variety. The following slight defects, however, may be allowed, provided these do not affect the general appearan ce of the produce, the quality, the keeping quality and presentation in the package: 
- slight defects in shape and colouring; 
- slight skin defects (corkiness, healed lenticels) and sunburn; the maximum total area should not exceed 4 cm2. 
The defects must not, in any case, affect the flesh of the fruit. 
The stalk, if present, may be slightly damaged.

sections.title: Class II
sections.text:
This class includes avocados which do not qualify for inclusion in the higher classes, but satisfy the minimum requirements specified in Section 2.1  above. The following defects, however, may be allowed, provided the avocados retain their essential characteristics as regards the quality, the keeping quality and presentation:  
- defects in shape and colouring; 
- skin defects (corkiness, healed lenticels) and sunburn; the maximum total area should not exceed 6 cm2. 
The defects must not, in any case, affect the flesh of the fruit. 
The stalk, if present, may be damaged.

sections.title: PROVISIONS CONCERNING TOLERANCES
sections.text: Tolerances in respect of quality and size shall be allowed in each package for produce not satisfying the requirements of the class indicated.

sections.text: For all classes, 10% by number or weight of avocados corresponding to the size immediately above or below that indicated on the package.

sections.title: Uniformity
sections.text: The contents of each package must be uniform and contain only avocados of the same origin, variety, quality and size. The visible part of the contents of the package must be representative of the entire contents.

page_spans.text: The contents of each package must be uniform and contain only avocados of the same origin, variety, quality and size. The visible part of the contents of the package must be representative of the entire contents.

sections.title: Packaging
sections.text:
Avocados must be packed in such a way as to protect the produce properly. The materials used inside the package must be new2, clean, and of a quality such as to avoid causing any external or internal damage to the produce. The use of materials, particularly of paper or stamps bearing trade specifications is allowed, provided the printing or labelling has been done with non-toxic ink or glue.
Avocados shall be packed in each container in compliance with the Code of Practice for the Packaging and Transport of Fresh Fruits and Vegetables (CXC 44-1995).

sections.title: Description of Containers
sections.text: The containers shall meet the quality, hygiene, ventilation and resistance characteristics to ensure suitable handling, shipping and preserving of the avocados. Packages must be free of all foreign matter and smell.

sections.title: CONTAMINANTS
sections.text:
7.1 The produce covered by this Standard shall comply with the maximum levels of the General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed (CXS 193-1995)
7.2 The produce covered by this Standard shall comply with the maximum residue limits for pesticides established by the Codex Alimentarius Commission.

7.2 The produce covered by this Standard shall comply with the maximum residue limits for pesticides established by the Codex Alimentarius Commission.

sections.title: HYGIENE
sections.text:
8.1 It is recommended that the produce covered by the provisions of this Standard be prepared and handled in accordance with the appropriate sections of the General Principles of Food Hygiene (CXC 1-1969), Code of Hygienic Practice for Fresh Fruits and Vegetables (CXC 53-2003), and other relevant Codex texts such as codes of hygienic practice and codes of practice.
8.2 The produce should comply with any microbiological criteria established in accordance with the Principles and Guidelines for the Establishment and Application of Microbiological Criteria Related to Foods (CXG 21-1997).

sentences.original:
The development and condition of the avocados must be such as to enable them:  
­ to withstand transport and handling; and 
­ to arrive in satisfactory condition at the place of destination.

classification.sentence:
The development and condition of the avocados must be such as to enable them:  
­ to withstand transport and handling; and 
­ to arrive in satisfactory condition at the place of destination.
classification.pattern: Subject + must be such as to + infinitive clause list

components.subject: The development and condition of the avocados
requirements: to arrive in satisfactory condition at the place of destination

classification.justification: Expresses obligation for development and condition to meet specified performance criteria, using 'must be such as to' structure.
notes: Normative performance obligation with compound infinitive conditions.

sentences.original:
Avocados can be sized through one of the following options: 
(a)  By weight of the fruit, in accordance with the following table: 
Size Code Weight (in grams) 
2 > 1220 
4 781 – 1220 
6 576 – 780 
8 456 – 576 
10 364 – 462 
12 300 – 371 
14 258 – 313 
16 227 – 274 
18 203 – 243 
20 184 – 217 
22 165 – 196 
24 151 – 175 
26 144 – 157 
28 134 – 147 
30 123 – 137 
32 80 – 123 (only Hass type)

sentences.original: Avocados in this class must be of good quality.

classification.sentence:
The following slight defects, however, may be allowed, provided these do not affect the general appearan ce of the produce, the quality, the keeping quality and presentation in the package: 
- slight defects in shape and colouring; 
- slight skin defects (corkiness, healed lenticels) and sunburn; the maximum total area should not exceed 4 cm2. 
The defects must not, in any case, affect the flesh of the fruit. 
The stalk, if present, may be slightly damaged.
classification.pattern: Permission modal phrase + condition clause + list of allowable defects with restrictions

components.conditions: provided these do not affect general appearance, quality, keeping quality, presentation
allowed_defects: slight defects in shape and colouring
slight skin defects (corkiness, healed lenticels) and sunburn with max total area 4 cm2
components.stalk_condition: if present, may be slightly damaged

classification.justification: Complex permission with condition limiting allowed defects; normative detail for tolerance in quality assessment.
notes: Multiple layered conditions and exceptions in permission modality.

sentences.original: Tolerances in respect of quality and size shall be allowed in each package for produce not satisfying the requirements of the class indicated.

classification.pattern: [Subject] + shall be allowed + [Scope] + for [Condition]

components.condition: for produce not satisfying the requirements of the class indicated

classification.justification: The sentence grants permission (shall be allowed) regarding tolerances with a specific scope and conditional clause, but no exact matching template exists. The pattern combines permission with conditional allowance tied to product quality and packaging context, representing a new pattern.
notes: The sentence uses 'shall be allowed' indicating formal permission, but linked to a conditional scope rather than a simple permission; this may overlap with obligation or condition categories but primarily expresses permission.

sentences.original: In addition to the requirements of the General Standard for the Labelling of Prepackaged Foods (CXS 1-1985), the following specific provisions apply:

classification.sentence: In addition to the requirements of the General Standard for the Labelling of Prepackaged Foods (CXS 1-1985), the following specific provisions apply:
classification.pattern: [Referential clause indicating baseline standard], [deictic phrase indicating added provisions], [main clause stating applicability of these provisions]

components.baseline_standard: requirements of the General Standard for the Labelling of Prepackaged Foods (CXS 1-1985)

classification.justification: The sentence imposes an obligation by stating that additional specific provisions apply beyond an established baseline standard. The structure is a referential introduction combined with a deictic phrase and a main clause of obligation. No known template matches this exact structural pattern, resulting in a new pattern classification.
notes: The sentence functions as a normative extension clause referencing another standard and adding obligations, which is distinct from typical obligation templates focused on direct action requirements.

sentences.original: Each package must bear the following particulars, in letters grouped on the same side, legibly and indelibly marked, and visible from the outside, or in the documents accompanying the shipment.

components.alternative_location: or in the documents accompanying the shipment

classification.justification: The sentence imposes an obligation with specific detail on the manner and location of marking that does not closely match any known template, resulting in a new structural pattern reflecting the detailed attribute list and alternative location clause.
notes: The presence of multiple qualifiers and alternative options increases complexity and differentiates this sentence from simpler obligation templates.

sentences.original: If the produce is not visible from the outside, each package shall be labelled as to the name of the produce and may be labelled as to name of the variety.

classification.sentence: If the produce is not visible from the outside, each package shall be labelled as to the name of the produce and may be labelled as to name of the variety.
classification.modality: Condition for the first clause; Obligation and Permission for the second clause
classification.function: Conditional obligation combined with conditional permission
classification.pattern: If [Condition], [Obligation] and [Permission] on the subject applies

components.Condition: If the produce is not visible from the outside
components.Obligation: each package shall be labelled as to the name of the produce
components.Permission: each package may be labelled as to name of the variety

classification.justification: The sentence has a conditional antecedent followed by two coordinated modal expressions: obligation and permission. This combination with conditional modality and mixed normative statements does not strongly match known templates and requires a new structural pattern to capture the conditional plus dual modality coordination.
notes: The sentence blends condition, obligation, and permission within a single coordinated consequent, which is less common in typical regulatory templates and thus lacks close template match.

8.1 It is recommended that the produce covered by the provisions of this Standard be prepared and handled in accordance with the appropriate sections of the General Principles of Food Hygiene (CXC 1-1969), Code of Hygienic Practice for Fresh Fruits and Vegetables (CXC 53-2003), and other relevant Codex texts such as codes of hygienic practice and codes of practice.
8.2

classification.sentence: The produce should comply with any microbiological criteria established in accordance with the Principles and Guidelines for the Establishment and Application of Microbiological Criteria Related to Foods (CXG 21-1997).
classification.pattern: Subject + modal verb (should) + verb (comply) + object (criteria) + relative clause specifying criteria source

components.subject: The produce
components.criteria_source: Principles and Guidelines for the Establishment and Application of Microbiological Criteria Related to Foods (CXG 21-1997)

classification.justification: The sentence imposes an obligation by requiring compliance with established microbiological criteria. Its structure features a subject followed by a modal verb and verb phrase describing compliance with specific criteria referenced by a guideline. No exact or close template from the catalog matches this specific compliance citation structure, resulting in a new pattern classification.
notes: The modal 'should' here conveys an obligation in normative regulatory context.
The sentence references an external standard for criteria but does not specify numerical limits, hence falls under obligation rather than limits and targets.

pages.title: STANDARD FOR AVOCADO
pages.summary: Página inicial com título do documento e cabeçalho, sem seções ou texto detalhado para hierarquia.

Página 2 do documento apresenta duas seções principais hierarquizadas: 'DEFINITION OF PRODUCE' e 'PROVISIONS CONCERNING QUALITY', incluindo subseções numeradas até o nível 3 com textos associados. A seção 'PROVISIONS CONCERNING QUALITY' e suas subseções continuam na próxima página.

A página contém três seções principais: duas subseções do nível 3 da seção 2.2 (Classes I e II), que continuam da página anterior, e uma nova seção nível 1 (3. PROVISIONS CONCERNING SIZING) iniciada nesta página e que continua para a próxima. Os textos estão associados aos seus respectivos títulos, respeitando a hierarquia e continuidade.

Página contendo capítulos 4, 5 e 6, cada um com suas subseções e textos associados adequadamente. Algumas subseções do capítulo 4.1 continuam da página anterior (4.1.2 e 4.1.3). A seção 6 continua para a próxima página.

A página apresenta as seções 6.2 (Particulars) com suas subseções detalhadas 6.2.1 a 6.2.5, seguidas das seções 7 (Contaminants) e 8 (Hygiene). A seção 6 é continuação da página anterior e prossegue para a próxima página, sendo marcada com is_continuation_from_previous_page=true e continues_on_next_page=true. Seções 7 e 8 começam e terminam nesta página.
